content: The tumor microenvironment (TME) comprises cancer cells and surrounding non-malignant cells that influence tumor behavior. Cellular components: immune cells (T-cells, B-cells, NK cells, macrophages, dendritic cells, myeloid-derived suppressor cells, neutrophils); stromal cells (cancer-associated fibroblasts, endothelial cells, pericytes, adipocytes). Non-cellular components: extracellular matrix (collagen, fibronectin, hyaluronic acid), cytokines, chemokines, growth factors, metabolites. TME classification: 'hot' tumors (inflamed, T-cell infiltrated, responsive to immunotherapy - melanoma, NSCLC, MSI-H cancers); 'cold' tumors (immune-excluded or immune-desert, poor immunotherapy response - pancreatic, prostate, glioblastoma). Immunosuppressive mechanisms: PD-L1 expression, T-regulatory cells, M2 macrophages, MDSCs, inhibitory cytokines (TGF-β, IL-10), metabolic suppression (adenosine, tryptophan depletion, lactate accumulation). TME evolves under treatment pressure, contributing to resistance.
